meaning: n.注视,沉思,预期,企图,打算
examples: He sat there deep in contemplation.
The archetypal extrovert prefers action to contemplation, risk-taking to heed-taking, certainty to doubt.
Even in the busiest museums there are many rooms and many pictures worth hours of contemplation which the crowds largely ignore.
By 1997 Delhi Metro's fathers were calling and full-time contemplation would have to wait.
Rub your chin in contemplation at the changing exhibitions inside the Charles H.
